chief_complaint: A 15-year-old boy presented with symptoms of Behcet's syndrome, including pulmonary embolism, pulmonary aortic aneurysm, and recurrent deep vein thrombosis.
history: The patient was diagnosed with Behcet's syndrome, a rare condition characterized by inflammation of blood vessels. He presented with pulmonary embolism, pulmonary aortic aneurysm, and recurrent deep vein thrombosis. He underwent a comprehensive strategy of systematic immunotherapy and anticoagulation treatment.
